faqs.question: How do I know if I have a clothes moth infestation in my Bayonne home?
faqs.answer: Signs of clothes moth infestation include small holes in wool clothing, carpets, or upholstery, silken tubes or cases in closets and drawers, adult moths flying in dark areas, and larvae or cocoons in fabric folds. You may also notice damage to natural fiber items stored in basements or attics. If you spot any of these signs in your Bayonne home, contact us immediately for an inspection.

faqs.question: What is included in your clothes moth control service?
faqs.answer: Our comprehensive service includes a thorough inspection to identify moth species and infestation extent, targeted treatments using safe yet effective products, heat or chemical treatments for infested items, recommendations for cleaning and prevention, and follow-up visits to ensure complete elimination. We customize our approach based on the severity of your infestation and your specific needs.

faqs.question: Why does clothes moth control cost between $550 and $5,000?
faqs.answer: Pricing varies based on the size of your Bayonne property, the extent of the infestation, the number of rooms or areas affected, and whether treatment requires specialized methods like heat treatments or fumigation. A small closet infestation in an apartment may cost around $550, while a severe whole-house infestation in a larger Bayonne home requiring multiple treatments could reach $5,000. We provide free estimates to give you an accurate quote.

faqs.question: How can I prevent clothes moths from returning after treatment?
faqs.answer: Prevention includes regularly cleaning and vacuuming closets and storage areas, storing woolens and natural fabrics in sealed containers or garment bags, using cedar products or moth repellents, maintaining low humidity levels, and regularly inspecting stored items for signs of moths. Our technicians will provide detailed prevention strategies specific to your Bayonne property during service, and we offer ongoing maintenance plans for continued protection.
